findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 33. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: The PDF has 'AGENDA' as a prominent title (visually large, centered, distinct), but in the HTML it is rendered as an h2 under the h1. While this is a reasonable choice, the sub-items under Chairman's Report are rendered as a bulleted list rather than indented items, which changes the visual hierarchy slightly. However, more notably, 'Thursday, May 14' is a clear section header in the PDF rendered as h3, which is acceptable. No significant hierarchy break detected beyond minor choices.
findings.evidence:
PDF: 'AGENDA' is a large centered title; HTML: rendered as

AGENDA

under

. This is an acceptable conversion choice.

findings.description: The footer text '* Times indicated are merely for guidance. The commission will proceed through the agenda until completed. **Probable Action Items' appears on both pages of the PDF (as page footers). In the HTML, it appears once in a footer element, which is correct consolidation. However, the 'Informational Materials' section is split into two bullet points in the HTML ('Informational Materials:' and 'Informational Materials continued:'), faithfully mirroring the PDF's page break presentation. This is not an error but worth noting that the page-break artifact is preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 ends with 'Informational Materials:' listing two sub-items; PDF page 2 starts with 'Informational Materials continued:' with more sub-items. HTML preserves both as separate bullets.
